fields.label: URL
fields.description: The URL of the Drone server, including `https://` and without trailing `/`

fields.label: Drone API token
fields.description: You can find your API token under <YOUR_DRONE_URL>/account

fields.label: Token
fields.description: Token for accessing the datasource API

groups.title: Connection
groups.description: Provide information to connect to the data source

instructions.msg: Drone configuration is stored under a service-keyed jsonData shape: jsonData.services.drone.* holds per-service settings and jsonData.variables.<name> holds connection variables, while secrets use flat dotted secureJsonData keys of the form "drone.<secret>". There is a single service with id 'drone'.

Authentication uses a Drone API token (bearer). jsonData.services.drone.auth.id is 'auth_bearer' (the backend defaults to it) and the token is the write-only secret at secureJsonData key "drone.token". You can find your API token under <YOUR_DRONE_URL>/account.

Connection: the server URL is built from the 'url' variable as {url}/api. Set jsonData.variables.url to the Drone server URL including https:// and without a trailing slash (e.g. https://drone.example.com). The single server is fixed, so no server selector is shown.

The secret at secureJsonData key "drone.token" is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether it is already configured, read secureJsonFields["drone.token"] == true rather than secureJsonData.
